title: Wave Function Collapse Studio
description: A real edge-socket WFC solver over hand-drawn tile sets — watch entropy-based collapse animate, then pin tiles and re-solve around them.
concept: A procedural tile-pattern generator built on a genuine Wave Function Collapse implementation: entropy-based cell selection, edge-socket compatibility propagation, and automatic backtrack-restart on contradiction. Choose from four hand-drawn tile sets (Circuit, Pipes, Maze, Knots), tune grid size and collapse speed, watch the pattern resolve tile-by-tile or solve it instantly, then click any settled tile to pin it and re-solve the rest of the grid around your constraint.
features: 4 built-in tile sets (Circuit, Pipes, Maze, Knots) each with its own edge-socket rule set and canvas-drawn tile style
Adjustable grid size from 8x8 to 40x40 via slider
Animated step-by-step collapse with play/pause plus instant-solve and reseed/regenerate
Click-to-fix: pin a tile at any cell and the algorithm re-solves/propagates around the lock, with automatic backtrack if pins conflict
Shareable URL hash (tileset, grid size, seed, theme encoded via btoa) plus localStorage autosave of last-used settings
8-slot save gallery with thumbnail previews and delete, stored as data URLs in localStorage
5 color themes affecting tile stroke/fill/pad colors live

interaction.mouse: Click a resolved tile to pin it in place (click again to release); drag sliders to change grid size / collapse speed; use the control dock to switch tile sets, themes, play/pause, regenerate, export, and save.
interaction.click: Click/tap any settled cell to lock its tile, then the grid automatically re-solves around the pin.
interaction.touch: Full touch support — tap cells to pin/unpin, drag sliders, tap buttons; mobile layout stacks the canvas above a compact control panel.
interaction.keyboard: Space toggles play/pause, R regenerates with a new seed, E exports the current pattern as PNG.
